description: Projects in this area examine how brain health, autonomic function, obesity, and mobility interact during stroke recovery.

details.label: Resources
details.value: NIH LRP and D-SPAN award materials, study protocols, clinical and neurophysiological measures

details.label: Technology used
details.value: Clinical assessment, neurophysiological testing, statistical modeling, reproducible analysis workflows

projects.summary: NIH-funded work examining how obesity may limit brain health and influence recovery after stroke
projects.title: Obesity, brain health, and stroke recovery

details.label: Technology used
details.value: Neuroimaging-informed measures, mobility testing, statistical analysis, ECG, exploratory multimodal analysis

projects.summary: Research exploring associations between central autonomic network integrity and mobility performance after stroke
projects.title: Dysautonomia and chronic stroke

title: Brain-Body Interactions in Recovery

description: Projects in this area use neuromodulation to understand lower-extremity sensorimotor plasticity and recovery.

details.label: Technology used
details.value: Transcranial magnetic stimulation, sensory augmentation, paired associative stimulation

projects.summary: Dissertation work characterizing lower-extremity neurophysiological responses to sensory augmentation in chronic stroke, alongside studies evaluating individualized paired associative stimulation as a tool to assay sensorimotor plasticity in healthy adults and people with chronic stroke
projects.title: Sensory motor integration & plasticity

details.label: Technology used
details.value: TMS, lower-extremity motor threshold assessment, neuronavigation

projects.summary: Work focused on lower-extremity motor threshold assessment, corticomotor excitability, and neuronavigated TMS methods that support individualized neuromodulation research after stroke
projects.title: Lower-extremity neurophysiology

title: Neuroplasticity & Neuromodulation

description: Projects in this area focus on measurement, function, and clinically meaningful outcomes in rehabilitation.

details.label: Technology used
details.value: Psychometric assessment, measurement validation, rehabilitation outcomes analysis

projects.summary: Development and validation of a balance measure designed to capture ability across the spectrum of functional mobility after stroke and other neurologic diagnoses
projects.title: Functional Balance Ability Measure

details.label: Technology used
details.value: Gait event detection, observational analysis, quantitative movement assessment

projects.summary: Work comparing observational gait analysis with quantitative methods for individuals with severe post-stroke gait impairment
projects.title: Gait analysis in severe post-stroke hemiparesis

details.label: Resources
details.value: Domer Research Award, Office of Student Research, Appalachian State University

projects.summary: Research examining ankle angular velocity, acceleration, and other movement variables as reliable measures related to post-injury clinical outcomes
projects.title: Lower-extremity biomechanics and clinical outcomes

title: Behavioral Outcomes Research
